steps.title: Identify what needs introducing
steps.content: After the blank, we have three items beginning with "the" and separated by commas: "the gathering of central themes and tensions," "the tossing of these elements into a dynamic narrative," and "the catching of the resolutions that emerge." These form a series that needs to be introduced.

steps.title: Choose the punctuation that introduces a series
steps.content:
"The author-as-juggler performs a routine" is a complete independent clause. What follows is a three-item list elaborating what the routine consists of. A colon after an independent clause correctly introduces that list.

A — "routine;": A semicolon must be followed by another independent clause. "The gathering of central themes..." is a noun phrase, not an independent clause. Fails.

B — "routine,": A comma does not signal the introduction of a formal list; it weakly connects and leaves the relationship between "routine" and the three items unclear. Fails.

C — "routine.": A period ends the sentence, then "the gathering of central themes..." becomes a sentence fragment — three noun phrases with no verb. Fails.

D — "routine:": The colon ends the independent clause and introduces the elaborating list of three parallel elements. Correct.
